rationale: Add a procedural hint to avoid long CLI args by using temp files/stdin, reducing Windows arg-length risk without touching other files.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.procedure.v1",
  "title": "windows_cli_arg_length_mitigation",
  "steps": [
    "Prefer writing large prompts/payloads to temp files and passing file paths instead of inline CLI args.",
    "Use stdin piping where supported to avoid command-line length limits.",
    "When invoking tools, keep arguments concise and reference files in memory/ or workspace paths."
  ],
  "applies_to": ["exec", "subprocess", "cli"],
  "notes": "Windows has strict max command line length; file-based IO is safer."
}

risks: Low risk: adds a new procedural note file only; no runtime behavior changes.
expected_benefit: Reduced failures due to Windows CLI argument length limits in future procedural decisions.
